revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:市町]]
'''Ichimachi''' (市町) refers to commercial districts that existed in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} prior to the modern era.
The term is believed to be related to the [[Tojosei|urban design system]] and the [[Higashi and Nishi Markets|Eastern and Western Markets]] established under it. It appears in the regulations on [[Market administrators|Ichishi]] in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, but there are differing interpretations: one theory suggests that it refers to the interior of a market,<ref>Nakamura, "Ichimachi," in *{{ill|Japanese Historical Encyclopedia|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Encyclopedia|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}*.</ref> while another suggests that it refers to the adjacent rows and blocks under the supervision of the ichishi.<ref>Kameda, "Ichimachi," in *{{ill|Dictionary of National History|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of National History|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}*.</ref> After the compilation of the *Engishiki*, the loosening of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} and changes in the urban environment of Kyoto, the then-capital, led to the formation of new commercial areas, such as those on Sanjō and Shijō in the upper capital, resulting in the decline of the original East and West Markets.
During the medieval period, the term "Ichimachi" came to refer to markets or commercial districts that emerged as commercial towns developed across various regions, typically established with shops. The merchants of these ichimachi formed a counterpart to itinerant merchants. By the early modern period, the term primarily referred to urban areas in general, contrasting with the {{ill|rural_area|en|rural_area|qid=Q175185|lt=countryside|1=Rural area}}.
== See Also == *{{ill|Market Settlements|ja|市場集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Market Settlements|1=market settlement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97706291}} - Settlements centered around markets in rural areas.
== Notes == <references/>
== References == * Kameda, Takayuki. "Ichimachi." In *{{ill|Dictionary of National History|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dictionary of National History|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6426904}}* 1 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1979). ISBN 978-4-642-00501-2. * Nakamura, Ken. "Ichimachi." In *{{ill|Japanese Historical Encyclopedia|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Historical Encyclopedia|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}* 1 (Heibonsha, 1992). ISBN 978-4-582-13101-7.
{{draft categories| [[ja:市町]]
}}
{{translated page|ja|市町|version=100023359|comment=Imported full ja history}}
